channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Cavity-mediated exciton hopping in a dielectrically engineered polariton system
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72043-1

Here, the authors report on local control of fiber-cavity exciton-polaritons in a laterally structured transition metal dichalcogenide monolayer. Local engineering of the semiconductor dielectric environment results in the formation of mesoscopic exciton domains, which induce a local polariton energy shift as a premise for the realization of polariton potential wells.
item.dc:title: Cavity-mediated exciton hopping in a dielectrically engineered polariton system
item.dc:creator: Lukas Husel
Farsane Tabataba-Vakili
Johannes Scherzer
Lukas Krelle
Ismail Bilgin
Samarth Vadia
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Iacopo Carusotto
Alexander Högele
